Learning Outcome: State the core acceptable-use principles (approved devices, least privilege, authorized purpose, ask-before-bypass) and give an example of each
Explain that CivicActions owns data on its systems and may monitor activity; articulate that there is no expectation of privacy on company resources
Distinguish authorized business use from permitted limited personal use; identify the boundaries (lawful, no interference, no unreasonable cost)
Identify at least five categories of prohibited behavior (illegal content, unapproved software, credential sharing, circumventing controls, data exfiltration) and explain why each is prohibited
List the baseline controls on managed laptops (FDE, EDR, firewall, patching, auto-lock) and explain why settings must not be altered
State the BYOD restrictions (no Confidential/CUI data), list the four minimum config requirements (screen lock, current OS, encryption, password manager), and describe when to report loss/theft
Apply workspace security practices: use WPA2/WPA3, lock screen, protect privacy in shared spaces, minimize printing, close privileged sessions when done

Classify data by sensitivity level and choose the correct sharing method (named sharing, approved channels only for Confidential/CUI); explain why "anyone with the link" is prohibited for non-Public data

Section: Cloud, Social Media, and External Communications
Learning Outcome: Identify approved vs. unapproved cloud services; state the rules for social media (Public info only, no speaking for company without authorization, no client disclosures)
Describe the process for requesting new software; identify the Prohibited Hardware and Software list; explain developer obligations for open-source (SBOM, license review, no secrets in code)
Use service identities (not personal accounts) for CI/CD; keep privileges minimal; avoid direct production changes; route changes through peer-reviewed pipelines
Locate the AI Usage Policy; identify what data may and may not be entered into AI tools
Protect accounts with MFA and password managers; lock/log out when leaving devices; keep high-risk sessions short-lived
Cooperate with endpoint protections; scan external files; never disable security agents; download only from trusted sources
Recognize reportable events (lost device, credential compromise, mis-sent data, suspicious email, malware); report immediately to security@civicactions.com; describe blame-free reporting culture
Understand the range of disciplinary actions (coaching through termination) and that sanctions are proportionate
Complete the policy acknowledgment at onboarding and after material changes
